question: He said to me, 'Are you coming with us?'
options: He asked me if I was coming with them.
He told me if I was coming with them.
He asked me if you were coming with us.
He asked me that was I coming with them.
answer: He asked me if I was coming with them.
explanation: For Yes/No questions, we use 'if' or 'whether'. The sentence structure changes from a question to a statement (Assertive). ✅

question: She said to him, 'What is your name?'
options: She asked him what was his name.
She asked him what his name was.
She told him what his name was.
She asked him that what his name was.
answer: She asked him what his name was.
explanation: In Wh- questions, the question word acts as the connector. The verb 'was' must come after the subject 'his name'. ✅

question: I said to her, 'Do you know me?'
options: I asked her if she knew me.
I asked her if she knows me.
I told her if she knew me.
I asked her that did she know me.
answer: I asked her if she knew me.
explanation: Simple Present (Do you know) changes to Simple Past (if she knew). ✅

question: They said to us, 'Where are you going?'
options: They asked us where we were going.
They asked us where were we going.
They told us where we were going.
They asked us where we are going.
answer: They asked us where we were going.
explanation: The word order shifts from 'are you' (question) to 'we were' (statement). ✅

question: He said, 'Will you help me?'
options: He asked if I would help him.
He asked if he would help me.
He said that will I help him.
He asked whether I will help him.
answer: He asked if I would help him.
explanation: Will ➔ Would. Pronouns change according to the context. ✅

question: She said to me, 'Did you see the movie?'
options: She asked me if I had seen the movie.
She asked me if I saw the movie.
She told me if I had seen the movie.
She asked me that had I seen the movie.
answer: She asked me if I had seen the movie.
explanation: Simple Past (Did you see) changes to Past Perfect (had seen). ✅

question: The teacher said to the boy, 'Why are you crying?'
options: The teacher asked the boy why he was crying.
The teacher asked the boy why was he crying.
The teacher told the boy why he was crying.
The teacher asked the boy why he is crying.
answer: The teacher asked the boy why he was crying.
explanation: The reporting verb 'said to' changes to 'asked', and the tense shifts to past continuous. ✅

question: I said to him, 'Can you lift this box?'
options: I asked him if he could lift that box.
I asked him if he can lift this box.
I told him if he could lift that box.
I asked him if he could lift this box.
answer: I asked him if he could lift that box.
explanation: Can ➔ Could, and 'this' ➔ 'that'. ✅

question: He said, 'When will the train arrive?'
options: He asked when the train would arrive.
He asked when would the train arrive.
He told when the train would arrive.
He asked when the train will arrive.
answer: He asked when the train would arrive.
explanation: Subject 'the train' comes before the auxiliary 'would'. ✅

question: She said to me, 'Have you finished your work?'
options: She asked me if I had finished my work.
She asked me if I have finished my work.
She asked me that had I finished my work.
She told me if I had finished my work.
answer: She asked me if I had finished my work.
explanation: Present Perfect (Have you finished) ➔ Past Perfect (if I had finished). ✅

question: Mother said to me, 'Where is your brother?'
options: Mother asked me where my brother was.
Mother asked me where was my brother.
Mother told me where my brother was.
Mother asked me that where my brother was.
answer: Mother asked me where my brother was.
explanation: Question order is replaced by assertive order. ✅

question: The policeman said to the man, 'Where do you live?'
options: The policeman asked the man where he lived.
The policeman asked the man where did he live.
The policeman told the man where he lived.
The policeman asked the man where he lives.
answer: The policeman asked the man where he lived.
explanation: Simple Present question ➔ Simple Past statement. ✅

question: He said to her, 'Whom do you want to see?'
options: He asked her whom she wanted to see.
He asked her whom did she want to see.
He told her whom she wanted to see.
He asked her that whom she wanted to see.
answer: He asked her whom she wanted to see.
explanation: Wh- word acts as the connector, followed by subject + V2. ✅

question: Ram said to Ravi, 'Are you coming to the party tonight?'
options: Ram asked Ravi if he was coming to the party that night.
Ram asked Ravi if he is coming to the party tonight.
Ram told Ravi if he was coming to the party that night.
Ram asked Ravi if was he coming to the party that night.
answer: Ram asked Ravi if he was coming to the party that night.
explanation: Tonight ➔ That night. ✅

question: The traveler said, 'Can anyone tell me the way to the inn?'
options: The traveler asked if anyone could tell him the way to the inn.
The traveler asked if anyone can tell him the way to the inn.
The traveler asked that could anyone tell him the way to the inn.
The traveler told if anyone could tell him the way to the inn.
answer: The traveler asked if anyone could tell him the way to the inn.
explanation: Can ➔ Could. ✅

question: She said to me, 'How is your father?'
options: She asked me how my father was.
She asked me how was my father.
She told me how my father was.
She asked me how is my father.
answer: She asked me how my father was.
explanation: Is ➔ Was (placed at the end). ✅

question: He said, 'May I use your telephone?'
options: He asked if he might use my telephone.
He asked if he may use my telephone.
He told if he might use my telephone.
He asked that might he use my telephone.
answer: He asked if he might use my telephone.
explanation: May ➔ Might. ✅

question: The interviewer said, 'Why did you leave your previous job?'
options: The interviewer asked why I had left my previous job.
The interviewer asked why had I left my previous job.
The interviewer asked why I left my previous job.
The interviewer told why I had left my previous job.
answer: The interviewer asked why I had left my previous job.
explanation: Simple Past (Did you leave) ➔ Past Perfect (had left). ✅

question: He said to me, 'Which book do you want?'
options: He asked me which book I wanted.
He asked me which book did I want.
He told me which book I wanted.
He asked me that which book I wanted.
answer: He asked me which book I wanted.
explanation: Simple Present ➔ Simple Past. ✅

question: She said, 'Shall I open the door?'
options: She asked if she should open the door.
She asked if she shall open the door.
She said that should she open the door.
She asked whether she would open the door.
answer: She asked if she should open the door.
explanation: When 'Shall' is used for an offer or suggestion, it often changes to 'should'. ✅

question: Anil said to his friend, 'How did you do that?'
options: Anil asked his friend how he had done that.
Anil asked his friend how did he do that.
Anil told his friend how he had done that.
Anil asked his friend how he had done this.
answer: Anil asked his friend how he had done that.
explanation: Simple Past ➔ Past Perfect. ✅

question: The lady said, 'What do you want, little boy?'
options: The lady asked the little boy what he wanted.
The lady asked what the little boy wants.
The lady told the little boy what he wanted.
The lady asked that what the little boy wanted.
answer: The lady asked the little boy what he wanted.
explanation: The vocative 'little boy' becomes the object of 'asked'. ✅

question: He said to me, 'Whose bag is this?'
options: He asked me whose bag that was.
He asked me whose bag was that.
He asked me whose bag this was.
He told me whose bag that was.
answer: He asked me whose bag that was.
explanation: This ➔ That. ✅

question: She asked, 'Are you happy here?'
options: She asked if I was happy there.
She asked if I am happy there.
She told if I was happy here.
She asked if was I happy there.
answer: She asked if I was happy there.
explanation: Here ➔ There. ✅

question: He said, 'How long will it take to reach there?'
options: He asked how long it would take to reach there.
He asked how long would it take to reach there.
He told how long it would take to reach there.
He asked how long it will take to reach there.
answer: He asked how long it would take to reach there.
explanation: Will ➔ Would. ✅

question: I said to him, 'Do you have a pen?'
options: I asked him if he had a pen.
I asked him if he has a pen.
I told him if he had a pen.
I asked him that had he a pen.
answer: I asked him if he had a pen.
explanation: Do you have (Simple Present) ➔ if he had (Simple Past). ✅

question: The teacher said, 'Who has broken this glass?'
options: The teacher asked who had broken that glass.
The teacher asked who has broken that glass.
The teacher told who had broken that glass.
The teacher asked that who had broken this glass.
answer: The teacher asked who had broken that glass.
explanation: Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect. ✅

question: He said to the servant, 'Why have you not cleaned the room?'
options: He asked the servant why he had not cleaned the room.
He asked the servant why had he not cleaned the room.
He told the servant why he had not cleaned the room.
He asked the servant why he has not cleaned the room.
answer: He asked the servant why he had not cleaned the room.
explanation: Have not ➔ Had not. ✅

question: She said to her mother, 'What shall I wear for the function?'
options: She asked her mother what she should wear for the function.
She asked her mother what she would wear for the function.
She told her mother what she should wear for the function.
She asked her mother that what she should wear for the function.
answer: She asked her mother what she should wear for the function.
explanation: When 'Shall' is used for asking advice, it changes to 'should'. ✅

question: The guide said to us, 'Have you seen the Taj before?'
options: The guide asked us if we had seen the Taj before.
The guide asked us if we have seen the Taj before.
The guide told us if we had seen the Taj before.
The guide asked if had we seen the Taj before.
answer: The guide asked us if we had seen the Taj before.
explanation: Present Perfect ➔ Past Perfect. ✅
